VAW.html:
The gist

SFVA inpatient medicine: 5 teams (1 resident + 2 interns), caps 16/team and 8/intern, with the Flow Hospitalist Service (FHS) as admission/logistics backup.

Day 1
  • Arrive 6:45–7:10am; take sign-out from the night intern (“FIREFLY”) by 7:10.
  • Team rooms: T1 1A-63 · T2 1A-94 · T3 1A-13B/C · T4 1A-68 · T5 1A-65 (door codes in the guide).
  • Schedule + days off live in the VA Box schedule, not QGenda. Sign-out doc: bit.ly/vaw-signout (open in Edge, logged into VA email).
Typical day
  • 7:15 holdover sign-outs → 8am code huddle outside 1A-62 → 8–8:50 morning report → 9–12 bedside rounds + MDR (Teams, bit.ly/VAW-MDR; slots 10:30–11:45) → 12–1 noon conference → afternoon tasks.
Call cycle
  • Long: 7am–9pm, admit cap 7 (decay 1 after 5pm); MOD phone forwards to your senior at noon; sign out to FIREFLY from 8pm, out before 9.
  • Then Post → Tiny (no new) → Short → Pre (7am–4pm, resident–intern day).
  • Non-call days: sign out to “DRAGONFLY” at 3pm (2pm weekends) once the checklist is done. Re-admits <7 days come back to the discharging team.
Tips
  • CPRS has no push alerts — call the RN for any urgent order (antibiotics, anticoagulation, AEDs). Vocera x27901.
  • Labs are drawn 0600/1300/2000 as “Lab Collect”; anything else is Ward Collect + call the RN (add-ons x22260).
  • Procedures always need a fellow/attending in the room — page FHS to supervise paras/LPs (pulm does thoras); order kits via RN text order >30 min ahead.
  • LST note within 72h of admission, and DNR needs an order, not just the note. Direct admits/transfers → page FHS. End notes with “I have discussed the case with my attending, Dr. ___…”.
Contacts

FHS 415-376-2034 (M–F 8–5, in-house 24/7) · MOD 443-7099 · FIREFLY/DRAGONFLY 443-7328 · ICU triage x27319 · RRT: dial 2222 · site director susie.wlodarczyk@ucsf.edu

VAICU.html:
The gist

Combined MICU/CCU, primarily a resident service: 3 residents (2 IM R2/R3 + 1 anesthesia R2) + 1-4 interns, plus a part-time NP. Team cap 15 (max 10 per senior). You are first call for MICU and CCU primaries, co-manage NSGY/ENT/Urology, and act as consultants for Gen/Vascular/CT surgery patients.

Typical day
  • 6:30 interns arrive and pre-round from the 6am distribution email + bit.ly/VA-micusignout (+ PICIS).
  • 8:00 code huddle outside 1A-62 (code pager handoff); 8:05 ICU didactics (schedule on Dr. Frank's door, 3A-53).
  • 9:00 rounds: new admits, then CCU, then MICU. 12-1 noon conference.
  • 15:00 short call resident/intern + flex intern leave; 16:00 PM rounds with MICU fellow & attending.
  • 19:30 night resident arrives and takes pending admits; long call out by 20:00 (long call intern may leave at 18:00 if census/acuity allow).
Call cycle & caps
  • Seniors: long call 7a-8p (4 new admits; protected from admissions 6:30-7:30pm), short call 7a-3p (3 admits — admits during rounds while long call leads), night 7:30p-8a (4 admits; sends the 6am distribution email and assigns pre-rounding).
  • Interns: long call 7a-8p, short call 7a-3p, flex 7a-3p — variable q2-q4 depending on staffing.
  • When the ICU caps, DRAGONFLY/FIREFLY start admitting per normal rules.
  • Days off per 28-day block: interns 4 (plus departmental didactic days), residents 5.
Admitting rules
  • Call the ICU fellow for ALL MICU admissions, rapids, and triages — even ones you decline. CCU admits/triages go to the cardiology fellow (call the CCU attending if no response in 15 min).
  • Neurology admissions are MICU admissions (neuro consults). For every GI bleed, also call the GI fellow ASAP and document the conversation.
  • Any patient who receives D50 or Narcan gets admitted to the ICU for monitoring.
  • Transfers out: write an ICU Interim Summary; call MOD before 5pm to find the accepting team. After 5pm (or if long call capped), ICU keeps the patient until AM holdover signout — signout is by the senior, never the intern.
Tips
  • No workroom — you sit at ICU computer stations with the RNs (no food/drink there).
  • PICIS / Critical Care Manager has vitals, drips, I/Os for ICU + TCU patients; log in with your CPRS codes and stick to "ICU Basic Flowsheet" and "Physician's View" (other views crash it).
  • All procedures require a fellow/attending at the bedside regardless of sign-off; transduce every new central line and get CXR after any neck stick.
  • Antibiotics are audited on the first choice — follow the VA section of the UCSF IDMP (idmp.ucsf.edu/guidelines-vasf).
  • Admission Med Rec note is required for admits from ER/CLC/outside hospitals. Deaths: call Donor Network West at 952-480-3100 within 1 hour; death note + discharge summary within 24h.
  • Ventilated or unstable patients leaving for imaging need an ICU resident with them; consult anesthesia first if >80% FiO2, PEEP >10, minute ventilation >15 L/min, or peak pressure >40.
Contacts

ICU triage x27319 · Rotation director James Frank James.Frank@ucsf.edu · PCCM chief Meshell.Johnson@ucsf.edu · CCU fellow: Amion code "ucsf" → Cardiology: VA, page via cw.ucsf.edu · ID consult 415-443-5151 · ICU pharmacy 415-313-9454

VAW Swing.html:
The gist

Swing resident ("DRAGONFLY"), 2pm-midnight. Takes afternoon signout from the day teams, cross-covers, and splits evening admissions 1:1 with the FHS hospitalist.

Shift flow
  • Arrive 2pm; earliest team signout is 3pm on weekdays, 2pm on weekends.
  • Code Whisperer 2-8pm — carry a code pager and back up the code leader at all codes.
  • Start admitting when long call caps or at 6pm, whichever is first. Admissions alternate 1:1 with FHS (they count med consults in the ratio).
  • Stop admitting at 8pm and take everything that arrives before 8 — Firefly is first up at 8pm. If Firefly is stuck in signout, place stable admit orders on 8-9pm arrivals.
  • If you cap before 8pm, FHS temporizes and saves admissions for Firefly.
Tips
  • List Med Team 6 and the FHS attending on your admit orders.
  • Discuss all new TCU admits with FHS; also staff any deterioration, unstable TCU patient, patient-directed discharge, or death before holdover presentation.
  • Send your H&P / discharge summary for co-signature to the FHS attending you staffed with.
  • Hospitalists are your backup — ask for help when you need it.
Contacts

X-cover pager 443-7328 · FHS via Careweb ("FHS") or 415-376-2034 · MOD 443-7099

VAW Night Flex.html:
The gist

Night resident ("FIREFLY"), 8pm-8am. Cross-covers the wards overnight, admits 1:1 with the hospitalist, and is the overnight Code Blue leader.

Shift flow
  • 8pm: take signout from long call first, then DRAGONFLY; take the code pager from long call and attend the 8pm code huddle outside 1A-62.
  • Take the MOD pager at 8pm — the 1:1 clock resets and you're up for the first admission (FHS counts med consults, and ICU admits if they cover the ICU, in the ratio).
  • Stop admitting at 6am; sign out to day teams by ~7:10 and leave after signing out your admissions.
Tips
  • Your admissions are your patients — keep advancing care through the night. List Med Team 6 + the FHS attending on admit orders.
  • Discuss all new TCU admits with FHS overnight (they document the discussion); staff deteriorations, patient-directed discharges, and deaths with the FHS attending.
  • No ICU transfers to Firefly — the ICU team keeps those patients and FYIs FHS for AM distribution.
  • Patient-directed discharge: complete the "Discharge Instructions" note so the PACT Handoff consult generates, then sign it.
Contacts

X-cover pager 443-7328 · FHS via Careweb ("FHS") or 415-376-2034 · MOD 443-7099

SFW.html:
The gist

ZSFG general medicine wards: 5 teams, each 1 resident + 2 interns (± AI/MS3s); cap 16/team, 8/intern; 5-day call cycle. House rule: “rounds are for learning.”

Day 1
  • Interns arrive 6:30am; cross-cover sign-out happens 7am in H6009.
  • Team rooms: Med 1 H4007 (x69520) · Med 2 H5011 (x69410) · Med 3 H5007 (x30653) · Med 4 H5013 (x69759) · Med 5 H6008 (x69649). Schedule on QGenda.
Typical day
  • 7am cross-cover sign-out (H6009) → 8:30 holdover sign-out in team room → rounds from 8:30 → MDR in H5009 (your team’s 11-min slot, 10–11am) → 12–1 noon conference → afternoon tasks.
Call cycle
  • Long: 7am–9pm, admitting window 12–6pm (cap 7, decay cap 2 pts 5–6pm); Swing takes 2 early admissions if you’re open for ≤2 at 1–3pm; 8–9pm sign out to “Octopus.”
  • Post / Tiny / Short: 7–5 (tiny/short: 3 holdovers, 0 fresh) · Pre: 7am–4pm.
  • Nights: “Crab” cross-cover intern (443-1903), “Lobster” night resident, Octopus. Non-call sign-out to Swing from ~3pm (2pm weekends).
Tips
  • Call ED pages back within 15 min — don’t block admissions. Readmits <7 days return to the prior team if a member remains.
  • Epic Chat is the primary MD–RN channel (answer within ~60 min; forward chats to Swing and mute notifications when you leave).
  • Meds to Bed: set pharmacy to “ZSFG Outpatient Pharmacy” (delivers 9:30am–6pm). Labs drawn 0400 and 1600.
  • Procedure supplies in H54 (Bldg 25); walk samples to the lab (Bldg 5, 2nd fl) — no tube system. Each team has a Butterfly ultrasound (login in the guide).
Contacts

Med MOD 443-5166 · Swing 443-6312 · cross-cover 443-1903 · FIS swing/night 443-3082 · chiefs’ office x61843 · site director rebeccah.brusca@ucsf.edu

SF ICU.html:
The gist

ZSFG medical ICU: 4 residents + 4 interns (one FM); resident call is q4 × 28h. You pre-round early — the machine starts at 6am.

Day 1
  • Interns arrive 6:30am to pre-round; the night resident’s holdover email with patient assignments lands at 6am.
  • Call rooms: MICU long call H3028, night float H3029 (badge access).
Typical day
  • 6am holdover email → 6:30 interns → 7:00 residents → 8:15 code huddle (H32) → 8:30 rounds → 12 noon conference → 5pm non-long-call teams sign out to the long-call intern → 8pm sign-out to the night resident (“Manta Ray”) → 9:15pm code huddle.
  • Caps: team 8, admitting 5; long-call resident is code leader until Manta Ray arrives at 8pm.
Tips
  • Confirm every admission with the charge RN (x60529) and use the Critical Care Orderset.
  • ICU triage note for every consult; staff with the fellow (x69412) or attending within 90 min.
  • Transfers to wards: warm bedside handoff with your senior — only to holdover or long-call teams, never to swing/overnight. Transfer summary + discontinue ICU-only orders.
  • Codes get a significant-event note (“.codeblue” dot phrase); consent notes go in the physical chart; death note = discharge summary within 24h.
Contacts

ICU MOD 443-2625 · intern pagers 443-6708 / 6723 / 6727 / 6730 · charge RN x60529 · fellow x69412 · director Carolyn Hendrickson (carolyn.hendrickson@ucsf.edu)

SFW Night Intern.html:
The gist

Night intern = Crab ("SFNFV Xcover"), 8pm-10am. First call for all non-Long-Call medicine teams, supervised by the Lobster (night medicine resident). Admit 0-2 patients after 11pm depending on bandwidth (Jul-Dec: 0-1; Jan-Jun: 1-2); nothing new after 4am.

Typical night
  • 8:00pm — with Lobster, take cross-cover sign-out from Swordfish in H6009; forward the X-cover pager to yourself; sign into Epic teams Med 1A-C through 5A-C (minus Long Call team).
  • 9:30pm — huddle with Octopus, Lobster, Kraken, and FIS Night.
  • 11pm-6am — cross-cover and admit with Lobster supervising.
  • 5:30am — send one-liners of your admissions to FIS via Epic Chat/secure email.
  • 6:30-8am — sign out to day teams in H6009; 8am forward X-cover pager (443-1903) to MOD (443-5166); 8:30-10am sign out holdovers to RIS teams in person (Lobster supervises all presentations).
Who's who at night
  • Lobster (night med resident): holds Med MOD from 9pm, admits 1:1:1 with Octopus and FIS Night (cap 5 including your admits), supervises everything you do.
  • Octopus (med/cards night resident): covers the Long Call team and admits medicine + cards floor.
  • FIS Night: in-house attending backup (pager 443-3082).
Tips
  • Give feedback on stale handoffs (outdated one-liners, thin contingencies).
  • Sign your notes to the RIS/FIS attending before you leave.
  • Overnight snacks/sandwiches in Resident Lounge GL18 (Bldg 5 ground floor).
Contacts

X-cover 443-1903 · Med MOD 443-5166 · Swing/Lobster 443-6312 · FIS Night 443-3082

SFW Night Flex.html:
The gist

Night intern = Crab ("SFNFV Xcover"), 8pm-10am. First call for all non-Long-Call medicine teams, supervised by the Lobster (night medicine resident). Admit 0-2 patients after 11pm depending on bandwidth (Jul-Dec: 0-1; Jan-Jun: 1-2); nothing new after 4am.

Typical night
  • 8:00pm — with Lobster, take cross-cover sign-out from Swordfish in H6009; forward the X-cover pager to yourself; sign into Epic teams Med 1A-C through 5A-C (minus Long Call team).
  • 9:30pm — huddle with Octopus, Lobster, Kraken, and FIS Night.
  • 11pm-6am — cross-cover and admit with Lobster supervising.
  • 5:30am — send one-liners of your admissions to FIS via Epic Chat/secure email.
  • 6:30-8am — sign out to day teams in H6009; 8am forward X-cover pager (443-1903) to MOD (443-5166); 8:30-10am sign out holdovers to RIS teams in person (Lobster supervises all presentations).
Who's who at night
  • Lobster (night med resident): holds Med MOD from 9pm, admits 1:1:1 with Octopus and FIS Night (cap 5 including your admits), supervises everything you do.
  • Octopus (med/cards night resident): covers the Long Call team and admits medicine + cards floor.
  • FIS Night: in-house attending backup (pager 443-3082).
Tips
  • Give feedback on stale handoffs (outdated one-liners, thin contingencies).
  • Sign your notes to the RIS/FIS attending before you leave.
  • Overnight snacks/sandwiches in Resident Lounge GL18 (Bldg 5 ground floor).
Contacts

X-cover 443-1903 · Med MOD 443-5166 · Swing/Lobster 443-6312 · FIS Night 443-3082

SFW Night.html:
The gist

Ward night float, 8pm-10am. The intern is the Crab ("SFNFV Xcover") — first call for all non-Long-Call medicine teams; the resident is the Lobster — holds Med MOD, admits 1:1:1 with Octopus and FIS Night (cap 5), and supervises all Crab admissions.

Typical night (intern)
  • 8:00pm — with Lobster, take cross-cover sign-out from Swordfish in H6009; forward the X-cover pager (443-1903) to yourself; sign into Epic teams Med 1A-C through 5A-C (minus Long Call team).
  • 9:30pm — huddle with Octopus, Lobster, Kraken, and FIS Night.
  • 11pm-6am — cross-cover and admit 0-2 patients with Lobster supervising (Jul-Dec: 0-1; Jan-Jun: 1-2); nothing new after 4am.
  • 5:30am one-liners to FIS → 6:30-8am sign out to day teams in H6009 → 8am forward X-cover pager to MOD (443-5166) → 8:30-10am sign out holdovers to RIS teams in person.
Typical night (resident)
  • 9:00pm — take over MOD from FIS Swing; receive sign-out on any moonlighter admissions.
  • Lobster takes the first medicine admission, then 1:1:1 with Octopus and FIS Night.
  • 5:30am one-liners to FIS → 7am forward MOD to Long Call senior → 8am supervise Crab's holdover sign-outs; sign notes to the RIS/FIS attending before leaving.
Tips
  • Give feedback on stale handoffs (outdated one-liners, thin contingencies).
  • Overnight snacks/sandwiches in Resident Lounge GL18 (Bldg 5 ground floor).
Contacts

X-cover 443-1903 · Med MOD 443-5166 · Swing/Lobster 443-6312 · FIS Night 443-3082

SFCC Night Flex.html:
The gist

Manta Ray = MICU night resident, 8pm-10am. Overnight code leader, primary cross-cover for non-Long-Call teamlets, ICU triage, admits up to 4.

Typical night
  • 8:00pm — arrive, forward pagers (MICU Admitting 443-2625, X-Cover 443-4339), take sign-out from the Long Call intern.
  • 9:15pm — code huddle in 32 ICU.
  • 10:00pm — evening rounds with charge RN (x60529) and the overnight fellow/attending; bring a WOW.
  • 6:00am — run the transfer-ready list with the charge RN, send one-liners to FIS Night, and send the ICU holdover distribution email (cc team attending, fellow, ZSFGCensusTracking@ucsf.edu).
  • 7:00am forward MOD to the Long Call senior; 8:30am sign out overnight admits and sign notes to the MICU attending before leaving.
Tips
  • The Long Call senior stays overnight as your backup — hand off cross-cover when you're actively admitting or triaging.
  • Before assigning holdovers, check Family Medicine and 7-day readmission rules.
  • Every triage consult needs a Significant Event note; staff with fellow (x69412) or attending within 90 min.
Contacts

MICU Admitting 443-2625 · MICU X-Cover 443-4339 · Charge RN x60529 · Fellow x69412

SF Card.html:
The gist

ZSFG inpatient cardiology, floor + CCU: 4 residents + 4 interns + a night resident (“Kraken”); q4 call. Chief of cardiology: Mary Gray; inpatient director: Jaya Mallidi.

Day 1
  • Interns arrive 6:30am to pre-round (holdover email lands 6am; residents at 7). Rounds in H6009.
Typical day
  • 8:15 code huddle (H32) → 8:30 rounds (H6009) → 11:30 cards MDR → 12 noon conference → 5pm all teams sign out to the long-call intern → 8pm long call signs out to Kraken.
Call cycle
  • Long: 7am–9pm; team cap 10, admitting cap 6; admit 7am–7pm (decay 1 pt 6–7pm, triage-only 7–8pm). Admits after 5pm are granted, then staffed next morning.
  • Post: no new (7–5) · Short: up to 4 holdovers incl. CCU (7–5) · Pre: 7–4.
  • Kraken (8pm–10am) takes all CCU admits overnight, cap 4; 9pm CCU rounds with the ICU charge RN, then calls the on-call cards fellow.
Tips
  • CCU admissions: call the ICU charge RN (x60529) for the bed first; CCU H&P + admission orders; the CCU fellow must hear about every CCU admission within 60 min and supervises procedures.
  • Consent notes go in the physical chart; death note = discharge summary within 24h. Cards has its own Butterflies (login in the guide).
Contacts

Cards MOD 443-3508 · Card 1–4 pagers 443-1712 / 1731 / 1738 / 1786 · x-cover 443-7196 · overnight 443-0395

SF Card Night.html:
The gist

Kraken = cardiology night resident, 8pm-10am. Holds Cards MOD, admits all CCU patients plus the first two cards floor admits (then 1:1 with Octopus; cap 4), cross-covers all cardiology patients, attends code blues to ensure ACLS is followed.

Typical night
  • 8:00pm — forward Cards MOD (443-3508) and team pagers; take sign-out from the Long Call team; CCU sign-out happens at the bedside.
  • 9:00pm — brief CCU rounds with ICU charge (x60529) and bedside RN; then call the on-call cardiology fellow together with the charge RN to review active issues and contingencies for every patient.
  • 9:15pm code huddle in 32 ICU; 9:30pm huddle with Octopus, Lobster, Crab, and FIS Night.
  • 6:00am — send the Cardiology holdover distribution email, marking CCU patients (cc team attending, CCU fellow, ZSFGCensusTracking@ucsf.edu).
  • 7am forward MOD to the Long Call senior; 8:30am sign out overnight admits and sign notes to the cardiology attending before leaving.
Tips
  • You are pulled from cards-floor admitting if total cards census is ≥25 or CCU census ≥5 — Octopus then admits floor patients alone; you keep the CCU.
Contacts

Cards MOD 443-3508 · Cards X-Cover 443-7196 · Cards Overnight 443-0395 · ICU charge x60529

SF Med|Card Night.html:
The gist

Octopus = night medicine/cards resident, 8pm-10am. Cross-covers the Long Call medicine team; admits medicine 1:1:1 with Lobster and FIS Night, and cards floor patients after Kraken's first two (then 1:1). Cap of 5 admissions.

Typical night
  • 8:00pm — take sign-out on the Long Call team and Swordfish admissions; forward the Swing pager; cards floor admitting starts at 8pm.
  • 9:00pm — medicine admitting opens; 9:30pm huddle with Lobster, Crab, Kraken, and FIS Night.
  • 5:30am — send one-liners: your medicine admits to FIS Night, your cards admits to Kraken.
  • 8:00am — sign out holdovers to RIS teams in person in Bldg 25 team rooms; if you admitted cards patients, join cardiology 8:30 teaching and sign out to the cards teams afterward. Sign notes to the accepting attending before leaving.
Tips
  • If total cards census is ≥25 or CCU census ≥5, Kraken is pulled and you admit all cards floor patients solo.
Contacts

Med MOD 443-5166 · Cards MOD 443-3508 · FIS Night 443-3082

SFW Swing.html:
The gist

Swordfish = swing resident, 12pm-12am. Cross-cover + admitting: supports resident-less teams with procedures, discharges, and sign-out; admits up to 3 patients 1:1 with FIS Swing (FIS takes the first); stops admitting at 8pm with a decay cap of 2 after 7pm. No ICU transfers to swing.

Typical shift
  • 12pm — arrive, forward the cross-cover pager to yourself, go to noon conference; hold MOD if Long Call capped before 1pm, then hand MOD to FIS Swing at 1pm.
  • 1-8pm — check in on teams working without a senior; take sign-out from med teams (from ~3pm weekdays, ~2pm weekends); admit.
  • If Long Call is open for ≤2 patients between 1-3pm, the Swing team takes 2 early admissions in sequence (1 to FIS Swing, then 1 to you); Long Call then admits until they cap or 6pm.
  • 8pm — sign out cross-cover to Crab and Lobster in H6009; wrap up admissions and sign them out to Octopus. Leave by midnight.
Tips
  • If you're signed off on a procedure, you are the designated procedural backup for other teams — notify FIS Swing when you're in a procedure so they cover MOD/admissions.
  • FIS Swing hospitalist (pager 443-3082, M-F 1-9pm, Bldg 10 2nd floor) is your clinical backup for questions and procedure supervision.
Contacts

Swing 443-6312 · X-cover 443-1903 · FIS Swing 443-3082 · Med MOD 443-5166

UCW.html:
The gist

Moffitt-Long general medicine: 8 teams (A–H), each 1 resident + 2 interns; team cap 16. You are the primary physician — pre-round on every patient daily, represent the team at MDR, keep the handoff current. Service directors: Adeena Khan, Molly Kantor.

Day 1
  • Sign-in from the night team (“Wombat”) before 7am; whole team present at 7am on call days.
  • Team rooms: A/C M1486 · B M980 · D/H M1556 · E M1493 · F M983 · G M987 (door codes in the guide).
Typical day
  • Interns start 6:30am. Long call 6:30am–9pm · post / post-post until 5pm · pre-call until 4pm.
  • Both daily conferences in M989 are expected.
Call cycle (q4)
  • Long: holdovers + admissions 7am–6pm — max 7 patients (6 fresh), decay cap 2 after 5pm; 7–9pm whole-team sign-out to Wombat.
  • Post: nothing new · Post-post: up to 2–3 holdovers (intern may be off/clinic/IHD) · Pre: no admits, resident or intern may be off.
  • Bounce-backs (<7 days) return to the original team any day; formal sign-out 8:30–9am.
Tips
  • Call the ED back within 15 min of the MOD page, eyes on the patient within 30 min, orders (Medicine Rapid Bed Request + Core IP Adult Admission) within the hour — goal is 60 min page-to-admission.
  • Re-triage questions go through your attending first (doesn’t count toward cap; email the CRs). Holdover algorithm lives on the DHM wiki.

UC MICU.html:
The gist

Parnassus MICU runs two teams — resident-staffed Blue and APP-staffed Gold. Days: 3–4 residents + 1 medicine intern + CCM fellow + attending. Nights: 1 resident ± 1 intern. Directors: Kathleen Liu (MICU), Wendy Smith (rotation).

Your night week
  • Shift 5pm–6:30am — arrive ~5pm for sign-out; night rounds start 9:30pm.
  • Admit all new MICU Blue patients until 6am, split 1:1 with the night resident; build the morning distribution on the board; sign out at 6am.
Your day week
  • Day team takes sign-in from the night resident by 6am. Holdover presentations are brief sign-outs — full plan gets discussed on rounds.
  • Resident long shift 6am–7pm holds the admitting pager until 5pm; short shift 6am–5pm.
  • Sign out before 5pm to the long-call resident; after 5pm to the night resident. Always check with the fellow before leaving.
Tips
  • The MICU fellow is notified of every admission; the attending is in-house and always on Voalte.
  • ICU transfers: the ICU team is primary immediately, even before the patient arrives — bedside sign-out within 30 min of the MOD page.
  • Notes: ICU writes the H&P on transfer in; on transfer out, ICU writes the progress/transfer note and the floor writes the H&P. Call room in 9ICU is reserved for the MICU resident.

UC CICU.html:
The gist

One mega CICU team: 1 attending + 1 fellow + 3 daytime providers (X/Y/Z — a mix of 1–2 APPs and 1–2 residents, individual cap 5) + 2 night providers. Documentation: Apex context "card crit care svc"; handoff lives under cardiology.

Shifts
  • Resident long 7AM–7:30PM: hold the CICU admitting pager + floor code pager 7AM–7PM; you distribute admits among the team (APPs and non-long-call expect to be told when they're up); individual cap 3 new admissions; hand off at 7PM.
  • Resident short 7AM–5PM: admit until 2PM, then PRN late-admitting backup; hand off 4:30PM, out at 5PM.
  • Night 7PM–7:30AM: cross-cover the X/Y/Z lists, triage + admit all new patients until 7AM (1:1 if two providers), build the morning distribution (holdovers preferentially to non-long-call), sign out at 7AM.
Admissions
  • For every ICU-bound patient, page the ICU triage fellow AND the CICU admitting pager simultaneously — the triage fellow assigns the bed; the CICU admitter assesses cardiac ICU needs and confirms acceptance with the CCU fellow.
  • Notify the CICU fellow of every new admission.
  • Sources: new CCU admits from the ED, upgrades from UC Cards, transfers from other services, and OSH transfers accepted by the cardiology attending.
Co-management
  • The ICU team exclusively writes/changes sedation, analgesia, and ventilation orders.
  • The CCU team manages everything else, including fluids and pressors.
Tips & contacts
  • Long-call resident hands the code pager to the WOMBAT at 7PM and takes it back at 7AM (M1015 workroom).
  • Workroom M1015; cards teaching M–F 8:30AM in M1179; a call room is always reserved for the CICU resident.
  • Cards + CICU tips & tricks doc lives on Box.

CICU admit pager 443-9197 · ICU triage fellow x39209 / 443-4985 · CICU attending 415-353-2428

UCW Night.html:
The gist

Moffitt night float is a zoo — literally: BAT (swing cross-cover + task/procedure help), FOX (overnight cross-cover of non-call teams), BADGER (R1 admitter) + WOLF (R2/3 admitter, supervises Badger), and WOMBAT (R2/3: long-call team cross-cover + code leader). Everyone works out of M1486.

Who does what
  • BAT 2PM–12AM: takes cross-cover from pre (3PM), post-post (4PM), and post (5PM) teams; hands cross-cover to FOX 8–9PM; backs up FOX until 11:30PM. Backup: SWAN.
  • FOX 8PM–7:30AM: cross-covers non-call teams overnight; signs day teams back in 6:30–7AM. Backup: WOMBAT + EAGLE (attending).
  • BADGER/WOLF 8PM–8:30AM: admit up to 7 overnight (max 3 Badger, 4 Wolf) until 6AM, then finish notes and present to day teams at 8:30AM. HAWK (attending) discusses plans after the 3rd admission and teaches.
  • WOMBAT 7PM–7:30AM: takes the code pager from the CICU long-call resident at 7PM, signs out both long-call teams 7–9PM (~1 hr each; teams out by 9PM), cross-covers them overnight, code-leads all non-ICU codes, supervises FOX after 11:30PM; code huddle 9:30PM in 10ICU.
Tips
  • Role task sheets for each animal are on Box; assigned call rooms exist for FOX and WOMBAT (codes from chiefs).
  • The code pager has the code-elevator key zip-tied to it; if the pager goes missing, tell the chiefs immediately (it has an Aeroscout tracker).
  • If a long-call patient is still in the ED waiting room at 7PM, the day team gives Wombat a warm handoff to finish admission tasks.

UCW Night Intern.html:
The gist

Intern-side night roles at Moffitt: FOX (overnight cross-cover of the non-call teams) and BADGER (R1 overnight admitter). Work room M1486 for the whole night menagerie; role task sheets are on Box.

FOX — cross-cover
  • Arrive 8PM; receive cross-cover sign-out from BAT 8–9PM; cover the non-call teams overnight.
  • Backup is the WOMBAT (R2/3; takes over FOX supervision from BAT at 11:30PM) and the EAGLE (attending).
  • If a code blue happens: WOMBAT code-leads — you take the long-call cross-cover pager and Voalte the night team that alternate FOX backup is needed.
  • 6:30–7AM sign the day teams back in; shift ends 7:30AM.
BADGER — admitting
  • Arrive 8PM; admit 8PM–6AM, max 3 admissions (the WOLF, an R2/3, takes up to 4 and directly supervises your admissions).
  • HAWK (attending) discusses high-level plans after your 3rd admission and provides overnight teaching.
  • 6–8:30AM: finish documentation; 8:30AM go to the team rooms and present your patients.
Tips
  • An assigned call room exists for FOX (code from chiefs).
  • Holdover presentations are brief: pertinent HPI, data, overnight course, plan skeleton — the day team builds the full plan on rounds.

UCW Night Admit.html:
The gist

BADGER (R1) and WOLF (R2/3) are Moffitt's overnight admitters. Wolf directly supervises Badger's admissions; HAWK (attending backup) discusses high-level plans after the 3rd admission and teaches overnight. Work room M1486.

Schedule
  • 8:00PM: arrive.
  • 8PM–6AM: admit — up to 7 patients total overnight (max 3 Badger, max 4 Wolf).
  • 6–8:30AM: complete remaining documentation and prep holdover presentations.
  • 8:30AM: go to the team rooms and present your patients.
Tips
  • Overnight admits are distributed to day teams via the holdover algorithm on the DHM wiki.
  • Holdover presentation = brief: pertinent HPI, data, overnight management, plan for the day — the receiving team finalizes the plan on rounds.
  • Role task sheet is on Box.

UCW Night X-Cover.html:
The gist

Cross-cover at Moffitt is split three ways: BAT (swing, 2PM–12AM), FOX (non-call teams overnight), and WOMBAT (long-call teams overnight + code leader for all non-ICU codes). All based in M1486; role task sheets on Box.

BAT (2PM–12AM)
  • Take sign-out: pre-call teams 3PM, post-post 4PM, post-call 5PM; cross-cover until 8PM.
  • 8–9PM: hand cross-cover to FOX, then back FOX up until 11:30PM (WOMBAT takes over after).
  • Proactively help teams with tasks and procedures (backup: SWAN, wards attending, or an on-service procedure attending).
FOX (8PM–7:30AM)
  • Cross-cover the non-call teams overnight; backup is WOMBAT + EAGLE (attending).
  • During a code: attend, take the long-call cross-cover pager from WOMBAT, and Voalte the night team that alternate FOX backup is needed.
  • 6:30–7AM: sign the day teams back in.
WOMBAT (7PM–7:30AM)
  • 7PM: pick up the code pager from the CICU long-call resident (M1015 workroom); hand it back at 7AM.
  • 7–9PM: sign out both long-call teams (~1 hr each — they must leave by 9PM); cross-cover them overnight and follow up their admits 9PM–12AM.
  • 9:30PM code huddle in 10ICU; 11:30PM take FOX supervision from BAT; code-lead all non-ICU codes.
  • 7–7:30AM: sign the long-call teams back in.
Tips
  • The code pager carries the code-elevator key (service elevators in Moffitt and Long) — keep it zip-tied on; missing pager → chiefs immediately.
  • Pager dead or stuck alerting? Swap the battery (10M nursing station has spares) or call IT 24/7.

IT Service Desk 415-514-4100

UC Cards.html:
The gist

5 UC cardiology teams: A and B are resident teamlets (1 resident + 1 intern each); C/D/E are APP teams, F is the cards hospitalist. The admitting pager is always held by the attending. Leadership: Atif Qasim, Chris Barnett.

Day 1
  • Workroom: M1015 conference room (clear out for MDR 2–3pm; 10-Long RN station is backup).
  • Sign in from the overnight hospitalist (“Crane”) by 7am.
  • Comms run on Microsoft Teams: “Cardiology Floor” (discharge planning — respond by 9am) and “CICU” (clinical).
Typical day
  • Teaching every weekday 8:30am in M1179.
  • Sign-out: to “Raven” (swing, 2–8pm) before 8pm, to Crane after; earliest recommended 3pm.
Call cycle
  • Long: admit 7am–7pm, team cap 10 (6 new), decay cap 2 after 5pm; interns 6:30am–9pm.
  • Post: no admits (to 5pm) · Short: up to 4 holdovers incl. bounce-backs (to 5pm) · Pre: to 4pm.
  • Crane takes all overnight admissions and cross-cover; “I-CON” med-consult attending (7am–7pm) for complex non-cardiac issues. Bounce-back = readmit or CICU transfer-out <7 days, returns to you.
Tips
  • Change first-call for holdovers right after sign-out.
  • Place “discharge referral to cardiology” orders ≥ 48h before discharge — one order per referral (EP + gen cards = 2); appointments then auto-appear on the AVS. Escalate discharge barriers to Chris Barnett.
  • Tips & tricks doc.
Contacts

CICU admitting pager 443-9197 · CICU attending 415-353-CICU (2428)

UC GI.html:
The gist

Inpatient GI consult service at Moffitt, working directly with the GI fellows, plus endoscopy exposure (EGD, colonoscopy, optionally ERCP/EUS). One trainee at a time — hence your solo slot.

Schedule
  • Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri AM: consults + outpatient procedures · Wed AM: GI Grand Rounds + fellows’ didactics · every PM: consults + inpatient procedures.
  • No call, no weekends, no holidays.
Day 1
  • Pre-rotation reading and service info: Box folder.
Expectations
  • See consults, present each case to the fellow and attending on call; write concise consult-focused H&Ps.
  • Core topics: GI bleeding, iron-deficiency anemia, N/V, diarrhea, IBD, constipation, ileus, abnormal GI imaging; indications/contraindications for common GI procedures.
Contacts

Attending: Max Brondfield (max.brondfield@ucsf.edu) · Admin: Svetlana Sogolova (svetlana.sogolova@ucsf.edu)

UC ONC.html:
The gist

For patients with a UCSF oncologist (or an easily reachable outside oncologist), the outpatient oncologist is the preferred first-pass for questions — but the inpatient oncology consult service is always available for any reason. Full (nuanced) guidelines live on Box.

Contacts
  • Workflow questions: sam.brondfield@ucsf.edu.

UC - Hepatology.html:
The gist

One of three GI consult services at Parnassus (alongside General GI and Interventional Endoscopy). Triage disputes between the GI services are resolved consultant-to-consultant, with one unified plan back to the primary team.

Scope
  • Liver disease evaluation and management: acute/chronic hepatitis (viral, autoimmune, metabolic, drug-induced), compensated and decompensated cirrhosis, abnormal liver tests without a structural lesion, HCC.
  • Portal hypertension complications: ascites, hepatic encephalopathy, varices, SBP.
  • Transplant assessment.
  • Procedures: liver biopsy; EGD/colonoscopy for patients followed in hepatology clinic or with an active hepatology consult (including their GI bleeds).

VAPQJ.html:
Heads up

Neither the VA guide nor the block manual describes the VAPQJ code — the VA guide is inpatient-focused. What carries over for an ambulatory block:

  • Ambulatory Report: Wed 8–9am in 1A-62. Ambulatory chief: Kenneth Hubbel.
  • Clinic onboarding: ADPAC Liliane Renous (liliane.renous@va.gov) sets up trainees for VA primary-care clinics.
  • PACT teams: find a patient’s PACT via the CPRS banner; message them on Teams for post-visit follow-ups.

VAAC.html:
Heads up

VAAC (VA ambulatory care) isn’t covered by the inpatient-focused VA guide or the block manual. The bits that apply:

  • Ambulatory Report: Wed 8–9am in 1A-62.
  • Clinic onboarding: ADPAC Liliane Renous (liliane.renous@va.gov).
  • PACT teams: identify a patient’s PACT via the CPRS banner; use Teams to arrange follow-up.
  • Site logistics (shuttle, PIV, food, rooms): see the dropdown on block 04.

UC LTU.html:
The gist

LTU = Liver Transplant Unit (Moffitt-Long): primary management of hepatology and liver-transplant patients. Team = senior resident + 2 medicine interns + 1–2 surgery interns, backed by GI / transplant-hepatology / transplant-surgery fellows. (The manual describes the senior’s role — you’d be one of the two medicine interns.)

Typical day
  • 7–8:45am pre-round / problem list → 8:45–9:45 rounds (Zoom) → 9:45–10 MDR → 10am–finish ICU + walking rounds.
  • Fri 7:30–8:30am transplant-surgery M&M · Tue/Thu 4:30pm HCC tumor board (as needed) · Wed midday transplant selection committee.
What you’ll learn
  • Decompensated cirrhosis management, transplant candidacy and evaluations, multidisciplinary care of complex liver patients on the floor and in the ICU.
Contacts

Attending: Courtney Sherman (courtney.sherman@ucsf.edu) · Admin: Jason San (jason.san@ucsf.edu) · LTU admission guidelines

Flex Night Intern.html:
The gist

UC night float is split into named roles; the intern night role is BADGER (admitting, directly supervised by the WOLF resident). All roles work out of M1486. (“Flex” assignments can differ — confirm your exact role with the chiefs.)

BADGER / WOLF
  • Arrive 8pm; admit 8pm–6am — up to 7 combined, max 3 for BADGER; 6–8:30am finish documentation; 8:30am present in team rooms.
  • HAWK (attending) discusses plans after the 3rd admission and teaches.
Who else is on at night
  • FOX (8pm–7:30am): cross-covers non-call teams; signs day teams in 6:30–7am.
  • WOMBAT (7pm–7:30am): cross-covers call teams, leads all non-ICU codes; takes long-call sign-out 7–9pm.
  • BAT (2pm–12am): proactive task/procedure help; hands cross-cover to FOX 8–9pm.
Tips
  • Role/task sheets on Box: BADGER/WOLF · FOX · BAT · WOMBAT.
  • The code pager carries a zip-tied key to the two code elevators. Dead pager → spare batteries at the 10M nursing station or IT (415) 514-4100. Missing pager → tell the chiefs immediately.
  • Code Blue orientation resources.

sites.VA:
